title: Patient-facing plain-language layer drafted from cited cards (education only; NOT medical advice; deferred out of v1; builds only if oncologist + advocate secured)
context: This is an OPTIONAL, HIGH risk-tier task that is deferred out of v1 by default. It builds ONLY if a credentialed oncologist AND a patient advocate have been secured (TO BE SECURED). The patient-facing plain-language layer provides educational summaries for patients and patient advocates derived solely from the cited, signed-off research cards. It is education only — it must never be read as treatment advice, therapy selection, or a clinical recommendation. The 'not medical advice' notice is mandatory on every page. No patient-facing release is permitted without the blocking oncologist + advocate sign-off recorded in task 016.
objective: Draft patient-facing plain-language summaries of the cited, scientifically-reviewed research cards: one summary per card, derived only from the signed-off card content, written for a non-specialist audience (patients and advocates), education-only framing throughout, 'not medical advice' notice on every page, routing clinical questions to a qualified oncologist, and nothing that reads as a treatment or trial recommendation. Submit drafts to the oncologist + advocate sign-off workflow (016).
acceptanceCriteria: GATE: this task does not start until a credentialed oncologist AND a patient advocate have been secured and their participation confirmed; if not secured, task remains open and the layer is skipped in v1
Plain-language summaries are derived only from the cited, version-scoped scientifically-reviewed research cards (011); no new claims or sources are introduced in the plain-language layer
Summaries are written for a non-specialist audience (patients and patient advocates); technical terminology is explained or replaced with plain language without losing scientific accuracy
Every page carries the mandatory 'Research summary compiled from open sources. Not medical advice. Not for clinical or treatment decisions. Consult a qualified oncologist.' notice prominently
No content reads as a treatment recommendation, therapy comparison, trial suggestion, or dosing guidance for any individual; summaries explain the research landscape, not what a patient should do
Clinical questions are explicitly routed to a qualified oncologist on every page
Drafts are submitted to the oncologist + advocate sign-off workflow (016) before any publication; the layer does not ship without recorded blocking sign-off
output: Draft patient-facing plain-language summaries (one per research card) committed to the repository as review drafts, ready for oncologist + advocate sign-off in task 016 — conditional on those reviewers having been secured beforehand.
